description_long: This sculpture depicts a stylized human figure in a seated or crouched position, supporting a large, flat, circular disk above its head. The figure has pronounced facial features, including a prominent nose and lips, and its arms are bent at the elbows, resting on its knees. The base is wide and flared, providing stability, while the disk at the top is thin and broad, possibly representing a platform or table surface. The surface of the sculpture is rough and matte, with a weathered, earthen appearance, suggesting it is made from fired clay or terracotta. The overall form is monolithic, with no visible seams or separate parts, and the coloration is a consistent earthy brown. The style is reminiscent of pre-Columbian or indigenous art, with simplified anatomical details and a focus on symbolic posture.
description: A rough, earthen brown sculpture of a seated human figure supporting a wide, flat disk above its head. The figure features stylized facial details and a broad, flared base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face, bent arms, and the disk supported above the head.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the curvature of the back, and the thickness of the disk.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back, shoulders, and the attachment of the figure to the base and disk.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile, arm position, and the overall silhouette of the sculpture.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure supporting a disk
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific environment.
